name: Roman Dam of Almonacid - Letux - Azuara — circular from Almonacid de la Cuba
name_original: Presa Romana de Almonacid - Letux - Azuara — circular desde Almonacid de la Cuba
region_intro: Touring cycling around Almonacid De La Cuba offers routes through a landscape characterized by historical sites, natural reserves, and agricultural areas. The region features varied terrain, including rolling hills and river valleys, with elevations ranging from gentle ascents to more challenging climbs. Cyclists can explore a mix of paved and unpaved roads, passing through olive groves and open countryside. Best touring cycling routes around Almonacid De La Cuba The most popular touring cycling route is Pueblo Viejo de Belchite y Reserva Ornitologica de El Planeron — round trip from Belchite, a 19.1 miles (30.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route explores historical ruins and a bird sanctuary. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Roman Dam of Almonacid - Letux - Azuara — circular from Almonacid de la Cuba, a moderate 15.2 miles (24.5 km) path. This circular route features views of an ancient Roman dam and passes through local villages. Local touring cyclists also love the Old Village of Belchite – Dirt Road Through Olive Groves loop from Belchite, a 42.4 miles (68.3 km) trail leading through extensive olive groves, often completed in about 6 hours 24 minutes. Touring cycling around Almonacid De La Cuba is defined by historical sites, natural reserves, and agricultural landscapes, with options available for moderate to difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Almonacid De La Cuba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Almonacid De La Cuba?
A: There are 5 touring cycling routes documented around Almonacid De La Cuba on komoot, offering a variety of experiences through historical sites, natural reserves, and agricultural landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Almonacid De La Cuba?
A: The region features varied terrain, including rolling hills and river valleys, with elevations ranging from gentle ascents to more challenging climbs. You'll find a mix of paved and unpaved roads, passing through olive groves and open countryside.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for different skill levels?
A: Yes, the routes around Almonacid De La Cuba cater to various abilities. There are 3 moderate routes and 2 difficult routes, ensuring options for both experienced cyclists and those looking for a more challenging ride.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31457313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Belchite – Dirt Road Through Olive Groves loop from Belchite&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 42.4-mile (68.3 km) trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera de los Navarros loop from Fuendetodos&lt;/a&gt;, covering 38 miles (61.2 km).

Q: What historical sites can I explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant historical landmarks. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Belchite&lt;/a&gt;, a poignant historical site, or cycle past the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268320" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Almonacid de la Cuba&lt;/a&gt;. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993382191/pueblo-viejo-de-belchite-y-reserva-ornitologica-de-el-planeron-round-trip-from-belchite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pueblo Viejo de Belchite y Reserva Ornitologica de El Planeron — round trip from Belchite&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores historical ruins.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, you can find beautiful vistas, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268320" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Roman Dam of Almonacid de la Cuba&lt;/a&gt;, which offers impressive views of the ancient structure and surrounding landscape. Many routes also traverse open countryside, providing expansive views.

Q: What natural attractions can I discover while cycling?
A: Beyond historical sites, you can encounter natural beauty like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3592739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de los Chorros&lt;/a&gt;, a river highlight, or the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2834036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Zafrane&lt;/a&gt; gorge. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993382191/pueblo-viejo-de-belchite-y-reserva-ornitologica-de-el-planeron-round-trip-from-belchite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pueblo Viejo de Belchite y Reserva Ornitologica de El Planeron — round trip from Belchite&lt;/a&gt; also includes a bird sanctuary.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Several routes are designed as circular loops, making them convenient for starting and ending in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994091194/roman-dam-of-almonacid-letux-azuara-circular-from-almonacid-de-la-cuba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Almonacid - Letux - Azuara — circular from Almonacid de la Cuba&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Zafrane loop from Puebla de Albortón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Almonacid De La Cuba?
A: Over 10 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Almonacid De La Cuba's varied terrain. They often praise the region's unique blend of historical sites, such as the ancient Roman dam, and the serene experience of cycling through extensive olive groves and open countryside.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through local villages?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994091194/roman-dam-of-almonacid-letux-azuara-circular-from-almonacid-de-la-cuba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Almonacid - Letux - Azuara — circular from Almonacid de la Cuba&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, passes through local villages, offering a glimpse into the regional culture and architecture.

Q: What is the typical duration for touring cycling routes in this area?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. You can find shorter rides like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993382191/pueblo-viejo-de-belchite-y-reserva-ornitologica-de-el-planeron-round-trip-from-belchite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pueblo Viejo de Belchite y Reserva Ornitologica de El Planeron — round trip from Belchite&lt;/a&gt;, which takes about 2 hours, up to longer, more challenging tours such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31457313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Belchite – Dirt Road Through Olive Groves loop from Belchite&lt;/a&gt;, which can take over 6 hours.
tips:
Almonacid de la Cuba viewpoint to see one of the most important and best preserved hydraulic works in Roman Hispania.
La Cuba, as this dam is popularly known, is one of the three that regulated the course of the Aguasvivas river, together with the Blesa and Muniesa dams.
Located on the outskirts of the Zaragoza town of Letux, we find this beautiful baroque hermitage that, thanks to the contributions of the neighbors, was restored after the Spanish Civil War.

The Church of Nuestra Señora de la Piedad was built in the second half of the 14th century in the style of a Mudejar fortified church, but it was modified in the 18th century. Now it is used for masses and is cared for by the 100 inhabitants. The 2 times that they have opened doors, the visitors have been open-mouthed with the interior.

Almonacid de la Cuba is a fascinating place to visit!
Roman Dam: The Roman Dam of Almonacid de la Cuba is the highest in the world built during the Roman Empire, at 34 metres high.
Church of Santa María: In the centre of the town is the parish church of Santa María, a construction from the second half of the 16th century with an imposing Mudejar tower.
Almonacid de la Cuba also offers various cultural activities that will allow you to immerse yourself in local life.
